skill.name: confluence-automation
skill.description: Automate Confluence page creation, updates, search, space management, labels, and hierarchy navigation through Rube MCP.
skill.summary: Use Rube MCP to manage Confluence pages, spaces, labels, searches, and page hierarchies.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution and cryptography issues, but review found they are Markdown inline tool names, parameters, and prose rather than executable code. The confirmed risk is medium because the skill instructs users to connect a third-party MCP endpoint to Confluence OAuth and includes write and delete-capable Confluence operations.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Endpoint Handles Confluence Access
medium_findings.description: Line 20 instructs users to add https://rube.app/mcp, and lines 23-25 establish a Confluence OAuth connection through Rube. This is legitimate for the skill but means Confluence content and account permissions pass through an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint and OAuth connection workflow are explicitly documented. The risk depends on granted Confluence scopes, but the external service dependency is clear.

medium_findings.title: Write-Capable Confluence Operations
medium_findings.description: The workflow includes creating and updating pages on lines 38-40 and creating spaces on line 100; the quick reference also lists page deletion on line 202. These actions are expected but can alter or remove workspace content if used without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed tool names directly describe content creation, updates, and deletion. They are normal Confluence automation features, so this is a controlled operational risk rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Matches Are Markdown Tool Names
low_findings.description: The many Ruby/shell backtick alerts are inline Markdown names for MCP tools and parameters, such as setup lines 15-16 and quick reference lines 191-208. No executable scripts, shell commands, or local command invocation were found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks are Markdown formatting around tool names and parameter names. The repository contains only SKILL.md, and no shell execution syntax appears outside documentation text.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts on lines 3, 49, 84, and 139 come from prose or parameter examples, not cryptographic code. No hashing, encryption, or digest algorithm use was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural-language descriptions or Confluence parameters. There is no implementation code that selects or invokes a weak cryptographic algorithm.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Line 36 describes searching Confluence content, and line 179 describes invalid XHTML behavior. No host reconnaissance commands, environment probing, or local system inventory behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation for Confluence workflows, not operating system inspection. No commands such as uname, whoami, ipconfig, or filesystem enumeration are present.

dangerous_patterns.title: External OAuth-Backed MCP Service
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on an external MCP server and a Confluence OAuth connection. Users should review the service and scopes before granting access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The setup section explicitly documents the MCP server URL and OAuth connection flow. The risk is inherent to the integration model, not hidden behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Destructive Workspace Operation Available
dangerous_patterns.description: The quick reference lists a page deletion tool. This capability should require explicit user confirmation before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The delete operation is only listed in the quick reference, but the capability is explicitly named. The impact is limited by Confluence permissions and user authorization.

content.value_statement: Confluence teams often lose time moving between search, page updates, labels, and hierarchy checks. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP workflows for structured Confluence automation.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for Rube MCP and Confluence OAuth connection checks.
Creates and updates Confluence pages with correct version handling.
Searches Confluence content by keyword or CQL with pagination guidance.
Lists, creates, and inspects Confluence spaces and space metadata.
Navigates child pages, ancestors, page versions, and labels.
Explains Confluence storage format requirements for page content.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active Confluence connection before workflows can run.
Does not include executable helper scripts or custom validation tools.
Requires current tool schemas because MCP tool parameters can change.
Confluence permissions, rate limits, and indexing delays still apply.

use_cases.title: Publish Project Documentation
use_cases.description: Create or update project pages, apply labels, and place pages under the correct parent in a Confluence space.

use_cases.title: Maintain Technical Knowledge Bases
use_cases.description: Search existing pages, fetch current versions, and update storage-format content without creating duplicates.

use_cases.title: Audit Space Structure
use_cases.description: Inspect spaces, page trees, labels, ancestors, and versions to understand documentation organization.

prompt_templates.title: Find Existing Pages
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Confluence automation skill to search for pages about <topic> in space <space key>. Show the most relevant matches and include page IDs.

prompt_templates.title: Create a New Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Confluence automation skill to create a page titled <title> in space <space key>. Convert this outline into Confluence storage format and add labels <labels>.

prompt_templates.title: Update an Existing Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Confluence automation skill to update page <page ID>. Fetch the current version first, preserve existing structure, and add this new section: <section content>.

prompt_templates.title: Map a Documentation Tree
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Confluence automation skill to analyze the child pages, ancestors, labels, and versions for page <page ID>. Summarize gaps and recommend cleanup actions.

output_examples.input: Search the DOCS space for onboarding API pages.
output_examples.output: A ranked list of matching Confluence pages with titles, page IDs, spaces, and notes about which result is most likely current.

output_examples.input: Create a release notes page under the Engineering Updates parent page.
output_examples.output: A created page confirmation with the title, parent location, labels applied, and a reminder to review formatting in Confluence.

output_examples.input: Review the page tree below the Platform Documentation homepage.
output_examples.output: A concise hierarchy summary showing direct children, ancestor context, label coverage, and pages that may need reorganization.

best_practices: Call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS before each workflow to confirm current tool schemas.
Fetch the current page version immediately before every update.
Ask for confirmation before creating spaces, updating important pages, or deleting content.
anti_patterns: Do not assume Markdown can be sent directly as Confluence page body content.
Do not update a page without first checking its current version number.
Do not grant broad Confluence OAuth access without reviewing the requested scopes.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Confluence account?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires an active Confluence connection through Rube MCP and permissions for the requested actions.

faq.question: Can it create and update pages?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents page creation and version-safe update workflows using Confluence MCP tools.

faq.question: Can it search page body content?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends Confluence CQL search with text queries for full-text content search.

faq.question: Does it convert Markdown automatically?
faq.answer: No. It reminds users that Confluence pages need storage-format XHTML content.

faq.question: Why is the security rating medium?
faq.answer: The skill uses a third-party MCP endpoint and can modify Confluence content. No malicious code was found.

faq.question: Should users confirm destructive actions?
faq.answer: Yes. Users should confirm updates, space creation, and deletion before the assistant calls those tools.
